title: NOC 82020 – Supervisors, mining and quarrying | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 82020 are the critical operational leaders managing the extraction of Canada's vast mineral wealth. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee complex underground and surface mining operations, ensuring that production targets are met while maintaining the highest safety and environmental standards. In 2026, as Canada accelerates the extraction of "Critical Minerals" for the global green energy transition, these supervisors are in exceptionally high demand. For immigration, this role is a prime candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level2Major Group82 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group820 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8202 – Contractors and supervisors, mining, oil and gasNOC Code82020

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers extracting coal, minerals, and ore.
Establish methods to meet rigorous work schedules and coordinate with engineering and technical personnel to resolve operational issues.
Oversee the safety of the entire mining or quarrying operation, ensuring compliance with provincial mining acts.
Resolve work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and resource recovery.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage equipment maintenance schedules.
Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures, and the operation of new mining technology.
Prepare production, safety, and incident reports for management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the occupations supervised (e.g., as a miner or heavy equipment operator) are mandatory.
Certification: Provincial certification as an underground mine supervisor or shift boss is often required (e.g., Ontario Common Core).
Education: A college or university program in mining technology or mining engineering is increasingly preferred for senior positions.
Strong leadership, mechanical aptitude, and the ability to work in remote environments (Fly-In-Fly-Out) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: High-level supervisors in remote regions often earn annual salaries exceeding $140,000, inclusive of remote premiums and performance bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector is entering a growth phase driven by the "Critical Minerals Strategy." With over 2,000 new supervisory positions expected through 2027 due to expansion and retirements, mining remains one of Canada’s most stable high-income career paths for skilled immigrants.
labour_market_demand: The demand for mining and quarrying supervisors is Very Good, particularly in northern and resource-rich regions. In 2026, Ontario (Sudbury/Timmins), Saskatchewan (Potash/Uranium), and Quebec (Abitibi-Témiscamingue) are facing significant labor shortages. With many senior supervisors reaching retirement age, Canadian mining companies are aggressively recruiting international talent with strong technical and leadership backgrounds to fill these essential roles.
Global Hub for Critical Minerals: Lead operations in a country that is a world leader in the sustainable production of nickel, potash, and uranium.
Technological Innovation: Manage the implementation of battery-electric vehicle (BEV) fleets and autonomous drilling systems in underground mines.
Safety Leadership: Work within some of the world’s most stringent mining safety frameworks, including the "Towards Sustainable Mining" (TSM) initiative.
Strategic PR Pathways: This occupation is a high-priority "Skilled Trade" that frequently receives lower CRS cut-off scores in category-based Express Entry draws.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted specifically for "Trade Occupations," making it easier for supervisors to receive an ITA.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure for workers already in Canada contributing to essential sectors like natural resources.
Saskatchewan INP (Occupations In-Demand): Frequently prioritizes supervisors in the mining and potash sectors.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based mining firm.
